{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_017fFNus3gJoqZJ1WPpZpDEN","type":"tool_result","content":"{\"content\":\"680:             run_functional_passes=False,\\n681:         )\\n682: \\n683:     graph_pass.apply(gm)\\n684: \\n685:     for _node in gm.graph.find_nodes(\\n686:         op=\\\"call_function\\\", target=torch.ops.higher_order.scan\\n687:     ):\\n688:         raise AssertionError(\\\"scan is not lowered to while_loop\\\")\\n689: \\n690: \\n691: @init_once_fakemode\\n692: def lazy_init():\\n693:     if torch._C._has_mkldnn:\\n694:         from . import decompose_mem_bound_mm  # noqa: F401\\n695:         from .mkldnn_fusion import _mkldnn_fusion_init\\n696: \\n697:         _mkldnn_fusion_init()\\n698: \\n699:     # Put this patterns in post-grad pass rather than joint-graph\\n700:     # pass since otherwise there will be perf/peak-memory regression:\\n701:     # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/148141\\n702:     register_replacement(\\n703:         # pyrefly: ignore [bad-argument-type]\\n704:         prepare_softmax_pattern,\\n705:         # pyrefly: ignore [bad-argument-type]\\n706:         prepare_softmax_replacement,\\n707:         [torch.empty(4, 8)],\\n708:         scalar_workaround=dict(dim=-1),\\n709:         # pyrefly: ignore [bad-argument-type]\\n710:         trace_fn=fwd_only,\\n711:         # pyrefly: ignore [bad-argument-type]\\n712:         pass_dicts=pass_patterns[1],\\n713:         extra_check=prepare_softmax_extra_check,\\n714:     )\\n715: \\n716: \\n717: def reorder_for_locality(graph: torch.fx.Graph):\\n718:     if torch.distributed.is_available():\\n719: \\n720:         def check():\\n721:             # This is a wait node, and `other_node`` is some collective node.\\n722:             # Eager semantics allow waits to be issued in a different order than\\n723:             # the collectives. Reordering this wait node might reorder collectives\\n724:             # which cause hangs. Once we have SPMD mode, we can safely reorder them.\\n725:             # However, increasing the locality between a collective and its wait node\\n726:             # is generally worse for performance.\\n727:             return node.target != torch.ops._c10d_functional.wait_tensor.default\\n728:     else:\\n729: \\n730:         def check():\\n731:             return True\\n732: \\n733:     def visit(other_node):\\n734:         if (\\n735:             other_node.op == \\\"call_function\\\"\\n736:             and other_node.target != operator.getitem\\n737:             and all((n in seen_nodes) for n in other_node.users)\\n738:             and get_mutation_region_id(graph, node)\\n739:             == get_mutation_region_id(graph, other_node)\\n740:             and check()\\n741:         ):\\n742:             # move node's producers right before it\\n743:             node.prepend(other_node)\\n744: \\n745:     seen_nodes = OrderedSet[torch.fx.Node]()\\n746: \\n747:     # only reorder nodes before the first copy_ in the graph.\\n748:     # )\n255:         )\n256:         collectives_bucketing = True\n257: \n258:     if collectives_bucketing:\n259:         # Fx collectives bucketing passes require topological sort for the cases:\n260: collectives in the bucket),\n265:         # that it is possible to reorder nodes to satisfy all ordering requirements.\n266:         #\n267:         # --- before bucketing ---\n268:         # in0 = \"\"\"\n477:     Given a list of symints/ints, this function returns a calculated expression of bound_symbols' values.\n478:     When we trace this function, we'll get a graph with call_function nodes that describes how the shape expr is\n479:     computed from bound_symbols' values.\n480: \n481:     Suppose shape = (s1*s2, s1+s2) and bound_symbols = {s1: arg0, s2: arg1}, the result will be\n482: to while_loop]\n506:     This pass decomposes `scan` to  `while_loop` by replacing the scan fx_node with a while_loop hop.\n507: \n508:     Suppose we have a function f:\n509: \n510:         def f():\n511:             init = torch.zeros([])\n512:             xs = torch.arange(4)\n513:             ys = []\n514:             for i in range(xs.size(0)):\n515:                 init = xs[i] + init\n516:                 ys.append(init)\n517: \n518:             # Return the final carry and stack the intermediates\n519:             return init, torch.stack(ys)\n520: \n521:     We could rewrite it with a scan with the benefits of reducing compilation time/binary size, reducing\n522:     memory usage, supporting loops over unbacked shapes and cudagraph etc.\n523: \n524:         def g():\n525:             def step_fn(init: torch.Tensor, x: torch.Tensor):\n526:                 next_init = x + init\n527:                 return next_init, next_init\n528: \n529:             init =      return loop_idx < xs.shape[0]\n545: \n546:             # we pre-allocate the output buffer ys and inplace\n547:             # copy the y of each intermediate into a slice.\n548:             # torch.fx.Graph):\n774:     if torch.distributed.is_available():\n775: \n776:         def check():\n777:             # This is a wait node, and `other_node`` is some collective node.\n778:             # Eager semantics allow waits to be issued in a different order than\n779:             # the collectives. Reordering this wait node might reorder collectives\n780:             # which cause hangs. Once we have SPMD mode, we can safely reorder them.\n781:             # However, increasing the locality between a collective and its wait node\n782:             # is generally worse for performance.\n783:             return node.target != torch.ops._c10d_functional.wait_tensor.default\n784:     else:\n785: \n786:         def check():\n787:             return True\n788: \n789:     def visit(other_node):\n790:         if (\n791:             other_node.op == \"call_function\"\n792:             and other_node.target != operator.getitem\n793:             and all((n in seen_nodes) for n in other_node.users)\n794:             and get_mutation_region_id(graph, node)\n795:             == get_mutation_region_id(graph, other_node)\n796:             and check()\n797:         ):\n798:             # move node's producers right before it\n799:             node.prepend(other_node)\n800: \n801:     seen_nodes = OrderedSet[torch.fx.Node]()\n802: \n803:     # only reorder nodes before the first copy_ in the graph.\n804:     # __init__(\n1667:         self, target: str, allow_outputs: bool = False, allow_inputs: bool = False\n1668:     ) -> None:\n1669:         \"\"\"\n1670:         Move constructors from cpu to the else ten.device\n1766: \n1767:     def get_cpu_indeg_count(self, graph: fx.Graph) -> dict[fx.Node, int]:\n1768:         \"\"\"\n1769:         Get the number of cpu inputs to a node\n1770:         \"\"\"\n1771:         cpu_indeg: dict[fx.Node, int] = Counter()\n1772: \n1773:         for node in graph.nodes:\n1774:             cpu_count = 0\n1775: \n1776:             def which constructors cannot be moved to gpu\n1897:         cannot_move_to_gpu = OrderedSet[fx.Node]()\n1898: \n1899:         # For any node in the graph, which constructors does it have a dependency on\n1900:         constructor_dependencies: dict[fx.Node, OrderedSet[fx.Node]] = defaultdict(\n1901:             OrderedSet\n1902:         )\n1903: \n1904:         # if a cpu node has a dependency on two different cpu constructors,\n1905:         # then if either constructor cannot be moved to gpu, the other cannot as well.\n1906:         # In this case any node with a dependency on one will have a dependency on the other\n1907:         equal_constructor_sets: dict[fx.Node, OrderedSet[fx.Node]] = {\n1908:             c: OrderedSet([c]) for c in constructors\n1909:         }\n1910: \n1911:         def make_dependencies_equivalent(\n1912:             set1: OrderedSet[fx.Node], set2: OrderedSet[fx.Node]\n1913:         ) -> OrderedSet[fx.Node]:\n1914:             # could use union find but not worth complexity here\n1915:             set1.update(set2)\n1916:             for obj in set1:\n1917:                 equal_constructor_sets[obj] = set1\n1918:             return set1\n1919: \n1920:         queue: list[fx.Node] = list(constructors)\n1921: \n1922:         for c in queue:\n1923:             constructor_dependencies[c].add(c)\n1924: \n1925:         while queue:\n1926:             node = queue.pop()\n1927:             dependencies = constructor_dependencies[node]\n1928: \n1929:             for user in node.users:\n1930:                 if self.cannot_be_moved(user):\n1931:                     cannot_move_to_gpu.update(dependencies)\n1932:                     break\n1933: \n1934:                 # this node was used on a op which takes in multiple devices and output a gpu\n1935:                 # tensor. we can convert its cpu input to gpu without making further changes\n1936:                 if self.allow_cpu_device(user) and self.is_on_target_device(user):\n1937:                     del cpu_indeg[user]\n1938:                 elif (\n1939:                     self.allow_inputs\n1940:                     and self.all_inputs_are_cpu_scalar_or_on_target_device(user)\n1941:                 ):\n1942:                     # this node takes only cpu scalar tensors or gpu tensors as inputs\n1943:                     # and outputs a gpu tensor. we can convert its cpu scalar inputs to gpu\n1944:                     # without making further changes\n1945:                     del cpu_indeg[user]\n1946:                 else:\n1947:                     # otherwise, we should continue look at its downstream uses\n1948:                     cpu_indeg[user] -= 1\n1949:                     if cpu_indeg[user] == 0:\n1950:                         del cpu_indeg[user]\n1951:                         queue.append(user)\n1952: \n1953:                 unioned_set = make_dependencies_equivalent(\n1954:                     dependencies, constructor_dependencies[user]\n1955:                 )\n1956:                 constructor_dependencies[user] = unioned_set\n1957: \n1958:         for node in cpu_indeg:\n1959:             if constructor_dependencies[node]:\n1960:                 cannot_move_to_gpu.update(constructor_dependencies[node])\n1961: \n1962:         all_cannot_move_to_gpu = cannot_move_to_gpu.copy()\n1963:         for constructor in cannot_move_to_gpu:\n1964:             all_cannot_move_to_gpu.update(equal_constructor_sets[constructor])\n1965: \n1966:         return OrderedSet(constructors) - all_cannot_move_to_gpu\n1967: \n1968: \n1969: def move_constructors_to_gpu(graph: fx.Graph) -> None:\n1970:     \"\"\"\n1971:     Moves intermediary tensors which are constructed on the cpu to gpu when safe\n1972:     \"\"\"\n1973: \n1974:     # cudagraph does not support cpu tensors. ...\n269:         # wait_ag0 = ag(in0)\n270:         # user0(wait_ag0)\n271:         # ...\n272:         # pre_in1 = ...\n273:         # in1 = transform(pre_in1)\n274:         # wait_ag1 = ag(in1)\n275:         # user1(wait_ag1)\n276:         #\n277:         # --- after bucketing ---\n278:         #\n279:         # in0 = ...\n280:         # user(wait_ag0) <--- wait_ag0 is defined only after bucketed collective.\n281:         #\n282:         # pre_in1 = ...\n283:         # in1 = transform(pre_in1)\n284:         # ag_bucket(in0+in1)\n285:         # wait_bucket\n286:         # wait_ag0 = wait_bucket[0]\n287:         # wait_ag1 = wait_bucket[1]\n288:         # user1(wait_ag1)\n289:         stable_topological_sort(gm.graph)\n290: \n291:     # Apply overlap scheduling if enabled\n292:     if config.aten_distributed_optimizations.enable_overlap_scheduling:\n293:         from torch._inductor.fx_passes.overlap_scheduling import (\n294:             schedule_overlap_bucketing_from_inductor_configs,\n295:         )\n296: \n297:         overlap_deps = config.aten_distributed_optimizations.insert_overlap_deps\n298: \n299:         # by default,
